long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Questions involving multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: The heating of NH₄NO₂ at 60–70°C and NH₄NO₃ at 200–250°C is associated with the formation of nitrogen containing compounds X and Y, respectively. X and Y, respectively, are
questions.sub_topic: Decomposition of ammonium nitrite and nitrate
questions.solution_summary: NH₄NO₂ → N₂ + 2H₂O (60–70°C); NH₄NO₃ → N₂O + 2H₂O (200–250°C). Thus X = N₂ and Y = N₂O.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure recall of decomposition products of ammonium salts. No mechanism involved.

questions.question_text: The correct order of the wavelength maxima of the absorption band in the ultraviolet-visible region for the given complexes is
questions.solution_summary: Strong field ligands cause larger splitting (Δ₀), absorb shorter wavelengths (higher energy). Order of ligand strength: CN⁻ > NH₃ > H₂O > Cl⁻. Therefore, [Co(CN)₆]³⁻ absorbs at shortest wavelength, [Co(NH₃)₆]³⁺ next, [Co(NH₃)₅(H₂O)]³⁺ next, and [Co(NH₃)₅Cl]²⁺ absorbs at longest wavelength.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparing ligand field strengths and understanding the relationship between Δ₀, wavelength, and colour. Moderate mechanistic understanding needed.

questions.question_text: One of the products formed from the reaction of permanganate ion with iodide ion in neutral aqueous medium is
questions.sub_topic: Permanganate oxidising action in neutral medium
questions.solution_summary: In neutral medium, MnO₄⁻ is reduced to MnO₂ and I⁻ is oxidised to IO₃⁻. 2MnO₄⁻ + H₂O + I⁻ → 2MnO₂ + 2OH⁻ + IO₃⁻.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires recall of redox products of KMnO₄ in neutral medium. Some mechanistic understanding of oxidation states.

questions.question_text: Considering the depicted hydrogen (H) in the hydrocarbons given below. The most acidic hydrogen (H) is
questions.solution_summary: Acidity depends on the stability of the conjugate base (carbanion). Aromatic and conjugated systems stabilise the negative charge better via resonance. The hydrogen attached to the most conjugated system is the most acidic.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of conjugate base stability through resonance and inductive effects. Deep mechanistic reasoning needed.

questions.question_text: Regarding the molecular orbital (MO) energy levels for homonuclear diatomic molecules, the INCORRECT statement(s) is(are)
questions.solution_summary: HOMO of F₂ is π-type (not σ). Bond energy of O₂⁺ is greater than O₂ (bond order 2.5 vs 2). Bond order of Ne₂ is zero (correct). Bond length of Li₂ is larger than B₂ (correct, Li has larger atomic size).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires MO diagrams and bond order calculations for multiple molecules. Deep understanding of MO theory needed.

questions.question_text: The pair(s) of diamagnetic ions is(are)
questions.solution_summary: La³⁺ ([Xe], f⁰) and Ce⁴⁺ ([Xe], f⁰) are diamagnetic. Yb²⁺ ([Xe]4f¹⁴) and Lu³⁺ ([Xe]4f¹⁴) are diamagnetic. La²⁺ and Ce³⁺ have unpaired f-electrons; Yb³⁺ and Lu²⁺ have unpaired electrons.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires recall of electronic configurations and magnetic properties of lanthanoid ions.

questions.question_text: For the reaction sequence given below, the correct statement(s) is(are) (In the options, X is any atom other than carbon and hydrogen, and it is different in P, Q and R)
questions.sub_topic: C-X bond properties and SN2 reactivity
questions.solution_summary: The C-X bond enthalpy follows R > P > Q. The nature of X (halogen) determines bond strength, with stronger bonds being less reactive towards SN2.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of bond properties and reactivity. Involves understanding of inductive effects and SN2 mechanism.

questions.question_text: In an electrochemical cell, dichromate ions in aqueous acidic medium are reduced to Cr³⁺. The current (in amperes) that flows through the cell for 48.25 minutes to produce 1 mole of Cr³⁺ is (Use: 1 Faraday = 96500 C mol⁻¹)
questions.solution_summary: Cr₂O₇²⁻ + 14H⁺ + 6e⁻ → 2Cr³⁺ + 7H₂O. To produce 1 mole of Cr³⁺, 3 moles of electrons are needed. Charge = 3 × 96500 C. Time = 48.25 minutes = 2895 seconds. Current = Charge / Time = 100 A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Stoichiometric calculation. Balance reaction, count electrons per mole of Cr³⁺, convert to current.

questions.question_text: At 25°C the concentration of H⁺ ions in 1.00×10⁻³ M aqueous solution of a weak monobasic acid having acid dissociation constant (Kₐ) of 4.00×10⁻¹¹ is X×10⁻⁷ M. The value of X is (Use: Ionic product of water (K_w) = 1.00×10⁻¹⁴ at 25°C)
questions.sub_topic: pH of weak acid with water autoionization
questions.solution_summary: For a weak acid HA with concentration C and dissociation constant Ka, considering water's autoionization, the total [H⁺] is determined by solving simultaneous equations for Ka and Kw. The derived [H⁺] is 2.24 × 10⁻⁷ M.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires deep understanding of simultaneous equilibria (acid dissociation + water autoionization). Must recognize that water contribution matters at very low acid concentrations.

questions.question_text: Molar volume (Vₘ) of a van der Waals gas can be calculated by expressing the van der Waals equation as a cubic equation with Vₘ as the variable. The ratio (in mol dm⁻³) of the coefficient of Vₘ² to the coefficient of Vₘ for a gas having van der Waals constant a = 6.0 dm⁶ atm mol⁻² and b = 0.060 dm³ mol⁻¹ at 300 K and 300 atm is (Use: Universal gas constant (R) = 0.082 dm³ atm mol⁻¹ K⁻¹).
questions.sub_topic: van der Waals equation and cubic equation
questions.solution_summary: Arranging the van der Waals equation as a cubic in Vm: PVm³ - (Pb + RT)Vm² + aVm - ab = 0. The ratio of the coefficient of Vm² to Vm is -(Pb+RT)/a. Substituting values gives -7.1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires manipulating van der Waals equation into cubic form, identifying coefficients, and calculating ratio. Heavy on mathematical reasoning.

questions.question_text: Considering ideal gas behavior, the expansion work done (in kJ) when 144 g of water is electrolyzed completely under constant pressure at 300 K is (Use: Universal gas constant (R) = 8.3 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹; Atomic mass (in amu): H = 1, O = 16)
questions.solution_summary: Electrolysis of 144 g (8 mol) of water produces 8 mol H₂ and 4 mol O₂, total 12 mol gas. Work done = -PΔV = -ΔnRT = -12 × 8.3 × 300 J = -29880 J = -29.88 kJ.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculate moles of water, moles of gases produced, then apply work formula. Counting heavy with some formula application.

questions.question_text: The monomer (X) involved in the synthesis of Nylon 6,6 gives positive carbylamine test. If 10 moles of X are analyzed using Dumas method, the amount (in grams) of nitrogen gas evolved is (Use: Atomic mass of N (in amu) = 14)
questions.solution_summary: The monomer for Nylon 6,6 is hexamethylenediamine (NH₂-(CH₂)₆-NH₂), which gives a positive carbylamine test. 10 moles of the monomer contain 20 moles of N atoms, which form 10 moles of N₂ gas. Mass of N₂ = 10 × 28 = 280 g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identify monomer (requires knowledge of Nylon synthesis), then count nitrogen atoms, then convert to N₂ gas.

questions.question_text: The reaction sequence given below is carried out with 16 moles of X. The yield of the major product in each step is given below the product in parentheses. The amount (in grams) of S produced is [Image with reaction sequence]. Use: Atomic mass (in amu): H = 1, C = 12, O = 16, Br = 80
questions.solution_summary: Starting with 16 moles of X and following the reaction sequence with specified percentage yields for each step, the final amount of product S produced is calculated to be 175 grams.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction chain with yield calculations at each step. Requires understanding of reaction sequence and stoichiometric calculations.

questions.question_text: The correct match of the group reagents in List-I for precipitating the metal ion given in List-II from solutions, is (P) Passing H₂S in the presence of NH₄OH (Q) (NH₄)₂CO₃ in the presence of NH₄OH (R) NH₄OH in the presence of NH₄Cl (S) Passing H₂S in the presence of dilute HCl Options: (1) Cu²⁺ (2) Al³⁺ (3) Mn²⁺ (4) Ba²⁺ (5) Mg²⁺
questions.solution_summary: H₂S in NH₄OH precipitates Group IV cations (Mn²⁺). (NH₄)₂CO₃ in NH₄OH precipitates Group V cations (Ba²⁺). NH₄OH in NH₄Cl precipitates Group III cations (Al³⁺). H₂S in dil. HCl precipitates Group II cations (Cu²⁺). So P-3, Q-4, R-2, S-1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure memorization of qualitative analysis group reagents and their cation precipitation. Matching groups to cations.

questions.question_text: The major products obtained from the reactions in List-II are the reactants for the named reactions mentioned in List-I. Match each entry in List-I with the appropriate entry in List-II. (P) Stephen reaction (Q) Sandmeyer reaction (R) Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction (S) Cannizzaro reaction
questions.solution_summary: Stephen reaction reduces nitriles to aldehydes. Sandmeyer reaction converts diazonium salts to aryl halides. Hoffmann bromamide degradation converts amides to amines (with one less carbon). Cannizzaro reaction is for aldehydes without alpha-H. The correct mapping is P-2, Q-4, R-1, S-3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure recall of named reactions and their reactant-product relationships. No reaction chains. No mechanistic reasoning.

questions.question_text: Match the compounds in List-I with the appropriate observations in List-II and choose the correct option. (P) Phenol derivative (Q) Amide (R) Anilinium chloride (S) Hydrazine derivative. Observations include reactions with phenyl diazonium salt, ninhydrin, FeCl₃, glucose, Lassaigne's test.
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry - Functional Group Tests and Reactions
questions.sub_topic: Compound identification based on reactions and tests
questions.solution_summary: P (phenol) reacts with FeCl₃ and ninhydrin. Q (amide) after hydrolysis gives amino acid which gives ninhydrin test but not phthalein dye test. R (anilinium chloride) gives blood red color with FeCl₃ in Lassaigne's test. S (hydrazine) gives hydrazones with glucose. Thus P-2, Q-5, R-4, S-3.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of multiple functional group tests and their observations. Matching compounds to their characteristic reactions.

questions.question_text: During sodium nitroprusside test of sulphide ion in an aqueous solution, one of the ligands coordinated to the metal ion is converted to (A) NOS⁻ (B) SCN⁻ (C) SNO⁻ (D) NCS
questions.solution_summary: During the sodium nitroprusside test for sulphide ion, the NO ligand is converted to NOS⁻, leading to the formation of a violet-coloured complex [Fe(CN)₅(NOS)]⁴⁻.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of ligand transformation during a test reaction. Pure mechanistic understanding of how NO converts to NOS⁻.

questions.question_text: The complete hydrolysis of ICl, ClF₃ and BrF₅, respectively, gives (A) IO⁻, ClO₂⁻ and BrO₃⁻ (B) IO₃⁻, ClO₂⁻ and BrO₃⁺ (C) IO⁻, ClO⁻ and BrO₂⁻ (D) IO₃⁻, ClO₄⁻ and BrO₂⁻
questions.solution_summary: Complete hydrolysis of ICl gives IO⁻ and Cl⁻. ClF₃ gives ClO₂⁻ and F⁻. BrF₅ gives BrO₃⁻ and F⁻.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires recall of hydrolysis products for three interhalogen compounds. Pure product enumeration.

questions.question_text: Monocyclic compounds P, Q, R and S are the major products formed in the reaction sequences given below. [Image]. The product having the highest number of unsaturated carbon atom(s) is (A) P (B) Q (C) R (D) S
questions.topic: Organic Chemistry - Reaction Mechanism & Product Analysis
questions.sub_topic: Major product formation in multi-step synthesis
questions.solution_summary: The reaction sequences lead to monocyclic compounds P, Q, R, and S. Compound S has the highest number (10) of unsaturated carbon atoms, making it the correct answer.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multiple reaction sequences (long chains) followed by counting unsaturated carbons in each product.

questions.question_text: The correct reaction/reaction sequence that would produce a dicarboxylic acid as the major product is [Image with options]
questions.solution_summary: The correct reaction sequence to produce a dicarboxylic acid involves bromination, dehydrohalogenation (KOH, EtOH), and strong oxidation (KMnO₄, H₂SO₄, Δ). This yields the dicarboxylic acid.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of multiple reaction sequences and identifying which sequence yields dicarboxylic acid.

questions.question_text: The correct statement(s) about intermolecular forces is(are) (A) The potential energy between two point charges approaches zero more rapidly than the potential energy between a point dipole and a point charge as the distance between them approaches infinity. (B) The average potential energy of two rotating polar molecules that are separated by a distance r has 1/r³ dependence. (C) The dipole-induced dipole average interaction energy is independent of temperature. (D) Nonpolar molecules attract one another even though neither has a permanent dipole moment.
questions.sub_topic: Types and properties of intermolecular forces
questions.solution_summary: Ion-ion potential is 1/r, ion-dipole is 1/r². Average potential for rotating polar molecules is 1/r⁶. Dipole-induced dipole interaction is temperature dependent. London dispersion forces are present in nonpolar molecules.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparing different types of intermolecular forces, their distance dependencies, temperature dependence. Multiple concepts must be compared and ranked.

questions.question_text: The compound(s) with P-H bond(s) is(are) (A) H₃PO₄ (B) H₃PO₃ (C) H₄P₂O₇ (D) H₃PO₂
questions.solution_summary: H₃PO₃ has one P-H bond. H₃PO₂ has two P-H bonds. H₃PO₄ and H₄P₂O₇ have no P-H bonds.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Pure structural recall of phosphorus oxyacids. Count P-H bonds in each.

questions.question_text: For the reaction sequence given below, the correct statement(s) is(are) [Image with reaction sequence]. (A) Both X and Y are oxygen containing compounds. (B) Y on heating with CHCl₃/KOH forms isocyanide. (C) Z reacts with Hinsberg's reagent. (D) Z is an aromatic primary amine.
questions.sub_topic: Reaction sequence analysis for X, Y, Z
questions.solution_summary: X and Y are oxygen-containing compounds. Z is an aromatic amine which reacts with Hinsberg's reagent. Options B and D do not correctly describe the products.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multiple step reaction sequence where products of each step need to be identified. Then statements about products need to be evaluated.

questions.question_text: In an electrochemical cell, dichromate ions in aqueous acidic medium are reduced to Cr³⁺. The current (in amperes) that flows through the cell for 48.25 minutes to produce 1 mole of Cr³⁺ is (Use: 1 Faraday = 96500 C mol⁻¹)
questions.solution_summary: Cr₂O₇²⁻ + 14H⁺ + 6e⁻ → 2Cr³⁺ + 7H₂O. 1 mole of Cr³⁺ requires 3 moles of electrons. Charge = 3F = 289500 C. Time = 48.25 min = 2895 s. Current = 100 A.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Stoichiometric calculation. Balance reaction, count electrons per mole of Cr³⁺, convert to current.

questions.question_text: At 25°C the concentration of H⁺ ions in 1.00×10⁻³ M aqueous solution of a weak monobasic acid having acid dissociation constant (Kₐ) of 4.00×10⁻¹¹ is X×10⁻⁷ M. The value of X is (Use: Ionic product of water (K_w) = 1.00×10⁻¹⁴ at 25°C)
questions.sub_topic: pH of weak acid with water autoionization
questions.solution_summary: For a weak acid HA with concentration C and dissociation constant Ka, considering water autoionization, the total [H⁺] is found by solving equations: Ka = [H⁺][A⁻]/[HA] and Kw = [H⁺][OH⁻]. The result is [H⁺] = 2.24 x 10⁻⁷ M.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires deep understanding of simultaneous equilibria (acid dissociation + water autoionization). Must recognize that water contribution matters at very low acid concentrations.

questions.question_text: Molar volume (Vₘ) of a van der Waals gas can be calculated by expressing the van der Waals equation as a cubic equation with Vₘ as the variable. The ratio (in mol dm⁻³) of the coefficient of Vₘ² to the coefficient of Vₘ for a gas having van der Waals constant a = 6.0 dm⁶ atm mol⁻² and b = 0.060 dm³ mol⁻¹ at 300 K and 300 atm is (Use: Universal gas constant (R) = 0.082 dm³ atm mol⁻¹ K⁻¹).
questions.sub_topic: van der Waals equation and cubic equation
questions.solution_summary: Arranging the van der Waals equation as a cubic in Vm: PVm³ - (Pb + RT)Vm² + aVm - ab = 0. The ratio of the coefficient of Vm² to Vm is -(Pb+RT)/a. Substituting values gives -7.1.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires manipulating van der Waals equation into cubic form, identifying coefficients, and calculating ratio.

questions.question_text: Considering ideal gas behavior, the expansion work done (in kJ) when 144 g of water is electrolyzed completely under constant pressure at 300 K is (Use: Universal gas constant (R) = 8.3 J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹; Atomic mass (in amu): H = 1, O = 16)
questions.solution_summary: Electrolysis of 144 g (8 mol) of water produces 8 mol H₂ and 4 mol O₂, total 12 mol gas. Work done = -PΔV = -ΔnRT = -12 × 8.3 × 300 J = -29.88 kJ.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Calculate moles of water, moles of gases produced, then apply work formula.

questions.question_text: The monomer (X) involved in the synthesis of Nylon 6,6 gives positive carbylamine test. If 10 moles of X are analyzed using Dumas method, the amount (in grams) of nitrogen gas evolved is (Use: Atomic mass of N (in amu) = 14)
questions.solution_summary: The monomer for Nylon 6,6 is hexamethylenediamine (NH₂-(CH₂)₆-NH₂). 10 moles contain 20 moles of N atoms, which form 10 moles of N₂ gas. Mass = 10 × 28 = 280 g.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Identify monomer (requires knowledge of Nylon synthesis), then count nitrogen atoms, then convert to N₂ gas.

questions.question_text: The reaction sequence given below is carried out with 16 moles of X. The yield of the major product in each step is given below the product in parentheses. The amount (in grams) of S produced is [Image with reaction sequence]. Use: Atomic mass (in amu): H = 1, C = 12, O = 16, Br = 80
questions.solution_summary: Starting with 16 moles of X and following the reaction sequence with specified percentage yields for each step, the final amount of product S produced is calculated to be 175 grams.
